title: Protecting Homeowners from Disaster Act of 2025
short_title: Protecting Homeowners from Disaster Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.
plain_summary: This bill changes tax rules to help homeowners who lose property in disasters. Right now, people can only deduct disaster losses on their taxes if the government declares it a federal disaster. Starting in 2025, this bill removes that limit so homeowners can deduct any personal property losses from disasters like fires, floods, or storms on their taxes. This means more families will be able to get tax help when they lose their belongings in disasters, even if the government doesn't officially call it a federal disaster.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protecting Homeowners from Disaster Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Repeal of Limitation on Deduction for Personal Casualty Losses
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to help homeowners who lose property in disasters. Right now, there are limits on how much money people can subtract from their taxes when they lose personal property in disasters like fires, floods, or storms. This law removes those limits by striking out paragraph five from section one hundred sixty five of the Internal Revenue Code. The change will apply to losses that happen in tax years that begin after December thirty first, twenty twenty four. This means starting in twenty twenty five, people will be able to deduct more of their disaster losses on their tax returns.
